channel.title: GrantOtter — Federal Funding Updates
channel.description: Aggregated weekly feed of grant opportunities and agency news from NIH, NSF, and DARPA.

item.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
item.description: Continuing agency efforts to bring space closer to home, NASA+ is heading to more streaming platforms. On Thursday, NASA announced its programming is on Fire TV Channels. Fire TV customers can easily access this content by asking Alexa+ on compatible devices. Future programming on Fire TV may include science mission launches, a test flight for […]

item.title: Crews Move Artemis IV Liquid Hydrogen Tank
item.description: Crews at NASA’s Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans transport the 130-foot-tall Artemis IV liquid hydrogen tank out of a production cell inside the main factory building into a detached test building on a separate portion of the 829-acre site on May 15, 2026. The liquid hydrogen tank will form part of the core stage for […]

item.title: NASA’s MAVEN Illuminates New Understanding of Auroras at Mars
item.description: NASA MAVEN (Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution) mission scientists have uncovered a key puzzle piece in understanding certain types of auroras on Mars, finding that they form in a similar way to Earth-based auroras. Results published Thursday in Nature Communications show the same mechanism that circulates and catapults charged particles into Earth’s atmosphere is happening at […]

item.title: NASA Astronaut Chris Williams Closes Out Space Station Mission
item.description: After eight months aboard the International Space Station for his first mission, NASA astronaut Chris Williams is preparing to return to Earth. During his assignment, Williams contributed to research for new cancer treatments, advanced the production of materials to improve computers and electronics, ventured into the vacuum of space to complete two spacewalks, and much […]

item.title: Maximizing the Value of Rigorous Research Through NIH-Funded Data and Biospecimen Repositories, with NIH Institute and Center Perspectives
item.description: NIH supports an array of data and biospecimen repositories to help ensure valuable scientific resources are available to researchers. Many repositories link biospecimens to rich clinical, phenotypic, imaging, genomic, or longitudinal datasets, allowing researchers to validate findings across data types and apply modern analytical approaches. Here, we discuss the policies and principles governing data management and repositories. Then, we spotlight selected examples of NIH-supported repositories.

item.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
item.description: Snow-capped mountains carved by deep river valleys preside over northwestern Washington state.

item.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
item.description: Media are invited to NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on Friday, July 31, to attend a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, the agency’s first new wind tunnel in more than 40 years. The event will include a brief media availability with: This event is in person only […]

item.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
item.description: Following its liftoff from Cape Canaveral on July 21 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, the Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) hosting the NASA-supported Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites (RSGS) payload is now en route to geosynchronous Earth orbit, where it will use its advanced robotics to service spacecraft. RSGS leverages in-space robotics expertise from NASA, […]

item.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
item.description: Editor’s Note: This advisory was updated on July 23, 2026, to reflect changes to the mission timeline.  NASA astronaut Chris Williams and Roscosmos cosmonauts Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikaev are wrapping up their 241‑day mission aboard the International Space Station. The crew and its Soyuz MS-28 spacecraft will undock from the orbiting laboratory’s Rassvet module […]

item.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
item.description: NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory unveiled this new look at the galaxy NGC 4736, also known as Messier 94, on June 30, 2026. Messier 94 is a spiral galaxy with a bright inner ring around it, called a starburst ring, where new stars are forming, perhaps fueled by gas driven in the unique oval-shaped structure seen […]

item.title: Mapping Io’s Hidden Heat With NASA’s Juno
item.description: Description This graphic illustrates the areas of Jupiter’s moon Io sampled by the Microwave Radiometer (MWR) instrument aboard NASA’s Juno spacecraft during two close flybys. The black overlapping lines show the instrument’s footprints during Perijove 57 on Dec. 30, 2023, when the spacecraft primarily mapped the northern hemisphere. The blue lines represent Perijove 58 on […]

item.title: Statement from NSF Chief of Staff Brian Stone, performing the duties of the NSF director, on advancing the Administration's AI priorities through the Genesis Mission
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation is proud to join the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, the U.S. Department of Energy and other federal partners in advancing the administration's artificial intelligence priorities through the…

item.title: New NSF initiative aims to unlock dataset value for AI-enabled scientific discovery
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation announced the NSF Unlocking Dataset Value for AI-Enabled Scientific Discovery program, a new investment to advance scientific community datasets and enable discovery and innovation using artificial intelligence…

item.title: NSF announces $83M investment in integrated data systems and services to advance AI-driven science and strengthen U.S. research infrastructure
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation today announced $83 million in awards through the Integrated Data Systems and Services (IDSS) program. The awards will expand access to data infrastructure resources that researchers can use alongside computing…

item.title: Considerations Before Contacting an NIH Program Officer: Practical Guidance Using Health Disparities Science as a Representative Example
item.description: Contacting an NIH Program Officer (PO) at the right time can be an important part of preparing a competitive research application.  Early communication helps establish a connection with NIH staff who understand your scientific area and the priorities of their Institute, Center, or Office (ICO).

item.title: Podcast: New treatment to fight deadliest cancer
item.description: U.S. National Science Foundation-supported professor Neil Forbes discusses how the bacteria salmonella can be modified for treatment against some of the deadliest cancers...

item.title: Request for Information: NIH Common Fund is Soliciting Ideas for NIH-wide Challenges and Opportunities
item.description: Notice NOT-RM-26-004 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Proposers Day: PENG
item.description: DSO will host a Proposers Day in support of the Protein ENGineering (PENG) program on Aug. 10 in Arlington, Va. | See Event

item.title: With personal protective gear, best defense is a good offense
item.description: DARPA support for startup turns technical promise into traction.

item.title: Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites lifts off
item.description: Historic Mission Robotic Vehicle en route to GEO, transforming space servicing.
